identity.title: Unlaminated Plastics Film and Sheet (except Packaging) Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers converting plastics resins into nonpackaging plastics film and unlaminated sheet for recurring or repeat external customers, including specification-bound industrial and specialty-film programs.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal plants, shells, non-control financing vehicles, plastics packaging film and sheet, plastics bags, laminated nonpackaging plastics sheet, rigid shapes, and activities classified outside NAICS 326113.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring and repeat B2B sales of film or sheet by roll, sheet, weight, or production order to industrial customers under product specifications; pricing reflects resin, conversion, performance, service, and qualification, with index-based or other cost pass-through arrangements in some contracts.

a.bridge: BLS measures the broader NAICS 326100 workforce, where production occupations are 58.94% of employment and extrusion operators alone are 4.83%; NIST identifies AI applications in inspection, maintenance, planning, inventory, documentation, and forecasting. I map high exposure to office, technical-document, planning, quality-analysis, and commercial tasks, moderate exposure to maintenance diagnostics, supervision, and vision inspection, and low exposure to physical line tending and material movement. Specialty-film firms may employ somewhat more technical and application work than broad plastics, but continuous extrusion remains production-heavy, so the proxy's net error direction is uncertain.
a.rationale: The exposed wage share sits mainly in production and demand planning, purchasing, quoting, specification and quality documentation, customer support, predictive maintenance, defect analysis, and machine-vision inspection. Physical extrusion, setup, changeovers, maintenance, testing, handling, and accountable release remain difficult to substitute.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted six-digit occupation table and plant task studies that separate already-automated control functions from current labor hours in engineering, application support, planning, quality, maintenance, sales administration, extrusion, and material handling.
caveats: BLS staffing data are for four-digit plastics product manufacturing, not NAICS 326113.
NIST use cases establish applicability but do not measure wage-weighted task exposure in nonpackaging film plants.
The injected compensation-to-receipts ratio uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and is harmonized, but it is not used to set this task-share primitive.

rho.bridge: NIST reports manufacturing-wide AI use, intended investment, applicable factory use cases, and common barriers. I translate those broad adoption signals into the five-year share of this industry's exposed opportunity that reaches stable operations, discounting for data quality, initial cost, skill gaps, cybersecurity, legacy integration, process validation, and customer qualification. The manufacturing survey likely includes larger and more digitally mature firms, so it probably overstates implementation for LMM plants.
rho.rationale: Planning, document, commercial, forecasting, and analytical workflows can be implemented relatively quickly, while validated machine vision, predictive maintenance, and closed-loop process changes require reliable plant data and integration. Specialty quality requirements and the cost of disrupting continuous lines limit full implementation.
rho.change_evidence: A three-to-five-year implementation cohort of LMM film and sheet manufacturers recording project scope, time to production, labor hours avoided, quality validation, sensor and MES readiness, adoption failures, and sustained use.
caveats: Reported use of AI tools such as chatbots is not equivalent to implementing a share of exposed wage opportunity.
Implementation will vary materially between modern instrumented lines and older assets with limited accessible data.

e.rationale: Independent firms in the EBITDA band commonly supply repeat film and sheet orders to external industrial customers and should fit the default lens. Eligibility is reduced by captive facilities, misclassification across packaging and laminated-sheet boundaries, firms outside the band despite dataset estimation, operations without stand-alone customers or financials, and businesses too concentrated or technically obsolete to be transferable.
e.change_evidence: A verified establishment-level list for NAICS 326113 enriched with ownership, product classification, captive status, recurring-order share, normalized EBITDA, customer concentration, and stand-alone financials.
caveats: No source directly measures eligibility for the frozen lens.
Public PE Films disclosures mix target nonpackaging surface-protection film with packaging overwrap film and do not reveal the LMM firm distribution.
The injected count of 72 LMM firms is an estimate built from size distributions and a margin bridge.

s5.bridge: Gallup reports that 22% of US employer-business owners plan to sell or transfer ownership within five years and that 52.3% of employer businesses are owned by people age 55 or older. I use the broad employer-business rate as the anchor and allow a modest upward base adjustment for succession pressure and buyer interest in qualified industrial capacity. Specialized assets and customer concentration can obstruct deals, while strategic consolidation can increase them; the proxy's net direction of error is ambiguous.
s5.rationale: Repeat programs, installed extrusion capacity, know-how, and customer qualifications can support a transferable operation, but intended sales may not close and internal corporate reorganizations do not qualify. Environmental, technical-obsolescence, customer-concentration, and equipment-condition diligence can shrink the buyer pool.
s5.change_evidence: A five-year census of closed US control transactions in independent nonpackaging film and sheet manufacturers, matched to the eligible-firm denominator and classified by seller age, buyer type, customer concentration, and whether the operating business transferred intact.
caveats: Gallup covers employer businesses across industries and sizes rather than NAICS 326113 LMM firms.
Owner plans are not completed transactions and do not isolate qualifying control transfers.
Divestitures of units inside larger companies may transfer assets without representing the eligible-firm succession process.

q.rationale: Specialty performance, patents, qualification, quality, uptime, and application support can let a supplier retain savings, particularly when benefits improve yield and service rather than only unit cost. Retention is constrained by concentrated customers, competitive repricing, resin-index or other pass-through mechanisms, and buyers' ability to demand productivity concessions. Tredegar's PE Films disclosures show both operating-efficiency benefits and favorable pricing, but also resin pass-through lags and extreme customer concentration.
q.change_evidence: Representative LMM customer contracts and bid histories showing resin indexes, cost-plus or open-book terms, renewal cadence, productivity-sharing clauses, qualification and switching costs, customer concentration, and margin behavior after documented automation projects.
caveats: A public specialty-film segment with four customers representing 88% of segment sales is not representative of every LMM firm or application.
Differentiated surface-protection film and commodity unlaminated sheet can have sharply different bargaining power.
Demand loss and implementation difficulty are excluded here and represented in d5, o, and rho.

d5.bridge: BLS projects real output for all plastics product manufacturing from 190.7 in 2024 to 212.4 billion chained 2017 dollars in 2034, roughly 1.1% annual growth; continuing that rate for five years implies about 1.056. I lower the target base slightly because nonpackaging film spans cyclical and technology-sensitive applications: Tredegar reported large surface-protection volume swings tied to the display cycle, inventory correction, and later restocking. The broad-plastics proxy may therefore overstate durable growth for exposed specialty applications, although other industrial end uses could outperform it.
d5.rationale: The current basket supports recurring protection and other industrial functions, but quantity can move with electronics, construction, automotive, agriculture, or other customer cycles, inventory policies, product transitions, downgauging, alternative processes and materials, and offshore competition. Diverse end uses and broader plastics output growth support a base modestly above current demand, with a wide range for mix and cyclicality.
d5.change_evidence: A US five-year constant-price, constant-quality volume forecast for NAICS 326113 split by end application, resin, gauge, and customer geography, with explicit treatment of inventory cycles, alternative material or process transitions, and announced domestic capacity.
caveats: BLS output covers all plastics products over ten years, not six-digit nonpackaging film and sheet over five years.
Tredegar's PE Films segment includes packaging overwrap and is unusually concentrated in high-technology surface protection.
Nominal sales and short-term inventory restocking are not direct measures of constant-price, constant-quality industry demand.

o.rationale: Surviving demand for physical film and sheet requires an accountable converter to formulate, extrude, control gauge and surface properties, test, qualify, and deliver material. Software may improve these activities but cannot supply the physical product. The downside permits customers to vertically integrate or replace current film functions with alternative processes, materials, coatings, or redesigned assemblies.
o.change_evidence: Interviews with major industrial film buyers and suppliers quantifying vertical integration, direct resin-to-product process changes, material substitution, coating or process alternatives, and current film volumes expected to be eliminated rather than sourced from an external manufacturer.
caveats: Demand eliminated by end-market contraction or material substitution is primarily captured in d5; o only addresses whether remaining demand still needs this kind of external accountable operator.
Integrated customer production and alternative processes are not measured for the whole six-digit industry.

narrative.executive_view: Nonpackaging film and sheet manufacturing is a repeat B2B physical-conversion business whose practical AI opportunity lies in planning, quality, maintenance, inspection, documentation, and customer workflows. Specialty qualifications can create stickiness, but continuous extrusion limits labor substitution and customer concentration, cost pass-through, cyclicality, and process or material transitions can erode retained value.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can improve demand and production planning, quoting, specification retrieval, quality-document generation, defect classification, machine-vision inspection, predictive maintenance, troubleshooting, inventory control, and customer service. Extrusion setup, continuous-line operation, maintenance, testing, handling, and accountable product release still require plant assets and skilled people.
narrative.value_capture: Savings may be retained through better yield, uptime, quality, throughput, and service, particularly for qualified specialty products. Retention weakens where contracts pass resin costs through, buyers are concentrated, suppliers rebid frequently, or customers can demand productivity concessions; the public specialty-film example displays both operational savings and strong buyer concentration.
narrative.firm_availability: The dataset estimates 72 firms in the LMM band, but the count is margin-bridged rather than observed. Many independent converters should fit the repeat external-customer lens, while captive capacity, classification ambiguity, technical obsolescence, customer concentration, and lack of stand-alone financials reduce the eligible and transferable pool.
narrative.demand_durability: Industrial and specialty-film functions recur as customers manufacture and ship their products, but the basket is heterogeneous and can be cyclical. Display-related surface-protection volumes have shown severe inventory and end-market swings, and some functions can transition to alternative processes or materials; diverse applications and projected broader plastics growth provide offsets.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Key uncertainties include the absence of six-digit occupational and implementation data, no direct eligibility or transaction denominator, widely varying specialty versus commodity economics, resin volatility, customer concentration, offshore competition, legacy equipment, end-market cycles, and technical substitution. A highly concentrated or obsolete product program can make otherwise capable assets unattractive.
narrative.principal_driver: Repeat, specification-bound physical supply and qualification requirements create recurring demand and multiple bounded workflows for AI-enabled operating improvement.
narrative.principal_weakness: Continuous-line labor limits substitution, while concentrated customers and cyclical or technically substitutable end uses can reclaim savings and destabilize demand.

sources.title: 2017 NAICS Definition File — 326113 Unlaminated Plastics Film and Sheet (except Packaging) Manufacturing
sources.supports: Defines the industry as converting plastics resins into nonpackaging plastics film and unlaminated sheet and identifies excluded adjacent activities.

sources.title: May 2023 OEWS Industry-Specific Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates: Plastics Product Manufacturing
sources.supports: Provides the broader-industry occupation mix, including 58.94% production occupations, 4.83% extrusion and drawing machine operators, and 5.17% inspectors and testers.

sources.title: The Rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in U.S. Manufacturing
sources.supports: Reports manufacturing AI adoption, expected growth, applicable use cases in quality, maintenance, forecasting and planning, and barriers including data, cost, skills, security, and legacy integration.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.supports: Reports employer-business owner age and broad US employer-business plans to sell or transfer ownership within five years.

sources.title: Tredegar Corporation 2024 Form 10-K
sources.supports: Describes surface-protection film applications, resin inputs and pass-through, customer concentration, demand cyclicality and inventory effects, favorable pricing, operating efficiencies, and manufacturing cost savings.

sources.title: BLS Employment and Output by Industry, 2024–2034
sources.supports: Projects broader plastics product manufacturing real output from 190.7 to 212.4 billion chained 2017 dollars over 2024–2034.
